does: This paper presents OpenAI's European Youth Safety Blueprint alongside its EMEA Youth & Wellbeing Grants program, outlining concrete initiatives designed to ensure AI systems are safe, responsible, and beneficial for teenage users, families, and educators across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. The work documents specific safety measures, governance frameworks, and funding mechanisms that OpenAI deployed to address digital wellbeing concerns among youth populations. It articulates practical approaches to implementing protective guardrails within AI systems while maintaining functionality, covering areas like content moderation, age-appropriate design, parental controls, and educator resources.
connects: This work builds on the broader movement toward responsible AI development and child safety online that emerged from years of social media research documenting harms to adolescent mental health and wellbeing. It responds to regulatory pressures from the EU's Digital Services Act and other EMEA region policies requiring tech platforms to demonstrate youth protection measures. The initiative extends prior work on AI safety and alignment by applying these frameworks specifically to the youth context, drawing from child development research and educational technology best practices, while also engaging with stakeholder input from families, schools, and child protection organizations.
enables: This blueprint and grant program establish foundational infrastructure for scaling youth-safe AI systems across diverse regional contexts. It points toward future work on age verification, adaptive safety controls tailored to developmental stages, and better measurement of AI's actual impact on youth mental health outcomes. The funding mechanism enables research into emerging risks from generative AI specifically for younger users, while the documented governance structures serve as templates for other AI organizations and jurisdictions. This work ultimately facilitates broader adoption of protective practices across the AI industry and helps establish accountability standards against which youth safety in emerging technology can be evaluated.
